response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior developer with strong full-stack and distributed-systems background, but the role specifically demands a C++ developer with deep STL, multi-threading, message-broker, IEC 62443, and industrial automation expertise. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015), with the bulk of recent work in TypeScript/Node.js/DLang. No evidence of MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, SDL/IEC 62443, or industrial cybersecurity work. Skills list does not even include C++.
strengths: Strong senior-level engineering experience across distributed, high-throughput systems (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of req/s)
Solid software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and documented technical communication
Some exposure to networking, async I/O, and event-driven architectures via DLang work
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience (~6 months at NI in 2014-2015); C++ not listed in current skills
No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No exposure to industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0) or industrial automation domain
